title: [Bug]: Mission metadata header parser accepts non-standard JSON constants
summary: Mission metadata headers reject non-finite float values during validation, but the parser still initially accepts Python-specific JSON constants such as NaN.
observed: A mission metadata header containing NaN is parsed before validation rejects the resulting float value.
expected: Mission metadata headers should reject non-standard constants as invalid JSON at the parsing boundary.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that passes a mission metadata header with NaN to parse_mission_metadata_header.
impact: Mission metadata is a cross-sink trust-boundary feature. Rejecting ambiguous JSON early keeps DLQ diagnostics clearer and prevents parser-specific behavior from leaking into sinks.
delivery_semantics: Invalid mission metadata remains a permanent validation failure. The core must still publish to DLQ before ACK when DLQ is configured.
security: The fix must not log the full header value, payload content, credentials, or private mission details.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves mission metadata rejects non-standard constants at parse time.
Valid mission metadata headers continue to work.
Existing mission metadata and sink mapping tests still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, mission metadata tests, Oracle mapping tests, file mapping tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and mission metadata documentation to clarify standards-compliant JSON parsing.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
